mission.title: kubeedge: Change to use gomod to manage the dependencies
mission.description: Change to use gomod to manage the dependencies. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubeedge deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubeedge version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeedge
helm list -n kubeedge 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubeedge installation.

steps.title: Review kubeedge configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeedge configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeedge
kubectl get configmap -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeedge
```
**What would you like to be added**:
Use gomod to manage the dependies and remove the vendor from the project

**Why is this needed**:
To manage the dependiences more efficient

steps.title: Apply the fix for Change to use gomod to manage the dependencies
steps.description:
We have also to increase the version of k8s.io/[kubernetes,api,...]. The kubernetes package include docker and I run into the same sirupsen error. 

Currently I use ``gotip`` instead of ```go```.  Gotip shows the include path, so we can identify the package which make problems. As an
```yaml
$go mod vendor
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/network latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/client latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/filters latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/strslice latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/container latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/image latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api latest
go: github.com/Sirupsen/logrus@v1.4.2: parsing go.mod: unexpected module path "github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
go: error load
```

steps.title: Upgrade kubeedge to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kubeedge:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubeedge kubeedge/kubeedge --namespace kubeedge
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge
helm list -n kubeedge
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeedge
kubectl get events -n kubeedge --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Change to use gomod to manage the dependencies" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
We have also to increase the version of k8s.io/[kubernetes,api,...]. The kubernetes package include docker and I run into the same sirupsen error. 

Currently I use ``gotip`` instead of ```go```.  Gotip shows the include path, so we can identify the package which make problems.
codeSnippets:
$go mod vendor
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/network latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/client latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/filters latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/strslice latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/container latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api/types/image latest
go: finding github.com/docker/docker/api latest
go: github.com/Sirupsen/logrus@v1.4.2: parsing go.mod: unexpected module path "github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
go: error loading module requirements

$ gotip mod vendor
go: github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/edge/pkg/edged imports
    k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/kuberuntime imports
    github.com/docker/docker/pkg/sysinfo imports
    github.com/Sirupsen/logrus: github.com/Sirupsen/logrus@v1.4.2: parsing go.mod: unexpected module path "github.com/sirupsen/logrus"

make[1]: Entering directory '/home/subpath/data/git/arbeit/kubeedge/edge'
go build cmd/edge_core.go
# github.com/google/cadvisor/http
../../../../go/pkg/mod/github.com/google/cadvisor@v0.29.2/http/handlers.go:101:33: too many arguments in call to prometheus.NewProcessCollector
	have (int, string)
	want (prometheus.ProcessCollectorOpts)
make[1]: *** [Makefile:5: edge_core] Error 2

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeedge installed or the issue environment reproducible.
